title: festuca woronowii subsp. caucasica
intro: Festuca woronowii subsp. caucasica, also known as Caucasus fescue, is a species of grass that belongs to the genus Festuca, which is a type of tufted perennial grass. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Festuca woronowii subsp. caucasica include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
